messages.content: How much does a 15-year-old get paid for working at McDonald's?

As an AI language model, I do not have access to specific information about wage policies of McDonald's or any other company. However, in many countries, the minimum wage for workers under the age of 18 is lower than that for adult workers. The amount a 15-year-old would get paid at McDonald's would depend on various factors such as their job responsibilities, hours of work, location, and local labor laws. It is always recommended to check with the local McDonald's restaurant or employment agency for specific information on the wage and employment policies for minors.
How much does a 15-year-old get paid for working at McDonald's in nyc

The minimum wage for workers under the age of 18 in New York City is the same as the standard minimum wage, which is $15.00 per hour as of 2021. However, the specific wage for a 15-year-old working at McDonald's in NYC would depend on their job responsibilities, experience, and the number of hours they work. It is best to contact the local McDonald's restaurant or employment agency to find out the specific wage policies for minors in NYC.
If I get paid 15 a hour and I work from 5 am to 5 pm how much would I make

If you get paid $15 per hour and you work from 5 am to 5 pm, that means you worked for a total of 12 hours. To calculate your total amount of pay, you can simply multiply your hourly wage ($15) by the number of hours you worked (12):

$15/hour × 12 hours = $180 

So, you would earn $180 for working from 5 am to 5 pm at $15 per hour. However, please note that this calculation does not take into consideration taxes, overtime, or any other deductions that may be applicable to your pay.
So I would be making 100+ a day

Yes, if you work for 12 hours at a rate of $15 per hour, you would earn $180 in a day. This means that you would be making more than $100 per day. However, please keep in mind that this calculation is based on the assumption that you are getting paid $15 per hour and that there are no other deductions from your pay. Actual pay may vary depending on various factors such as taxes, location, position, experience, etc.
What if I got paid 20 dollars

If you get paid $20 per hour and you work for 12 hours from 5 am to 5 pm, you would earn a total of $240 for the day. 

You can calculate it by simply multiplying your hourly rate ($20) by the numbers of hours worked (12):

$20/hour × 12 hours = $240 

So, if you get paid $20 per hour, you would earn more money than if you get paid $15 per hour for the same length of shift. However, please note that this calculation does not consider other factors such as taxes, overtime pay, deductions, etc.
Can you deduct the taxes as a example

Sure, I can provide an example of how taxes would affect your pay.

Let's say you get paid $20 per hour and work for 12 hours a day from 5 am to 5 pm. Assuming a combined federal and state tax rate of 25%, your take-home pay would be reduced by 25% due to taxes. Therefore, your actual net pay (after taxes) would be:

Gross pay = $20/hour × 12 hours = $240
Total taxes = Gross pay x Tax rate = $240 x 25% = $60
Net pay = Gross pay - Total taxes = $240 - $60 = $180

So, if the combined federal and state tax rate is 25%, your actual net pay for working 12 hours at $20 per hour would be $180 per day. Please note that this is just an example, and actual tax rates may vary depending on your income, location, and other factors.
Okay can you do the same for 180 now

Sure, I can provide an example for $180 as well.

Assuming that you earn $180 for working 12 hours at $15 per hour, let's say the combined federal and state tax rate is 20%. Your actual net pay (after taxes) would be:

Gross pay = $15/hour × 12 hours = $180
Total taxes = Gross pay x Tax rate = $180 x 20% = $36
Net pay = Gross pay - Total taxes = $180 - $36 = $144

So, if the combined federal and state tax rate is 20%, your actual net pay for working 12 hours at $15 per hour would be around $144 per day after tax deductions. Again, please note that tax rates vary and this is just an example to help understand how taxes might affect your net pay.
Is there anyway to get tax free pay

As an AI language model, I cannot provide tax or financial advice, and it is always recommended to consult with a tax professional or financial advisor about any specific tax-related questions you may have. However, it's worth noting that in most countries, paying taxes is mandatory, and it is illegal to evade or avoid taxes. That being said, there may be some deductions or credits you may be eligible for that can reduce the amount of taxes you owe. This can depend on various factors such as your income level, dependents, and expenses. But again, if you have any specific questions on this topic, it is always best to consult with a qualified tax professional or financial advisor.
If I’m a minor does my paycheck from McDonald’s come tax free

In general, minimum age requirements do not exempt you from federal, state, and local taxes on your earnings, even if you are a minor. The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) requires all employees, including minors, to pay federal income taxes if they earn above a certain amount. 

In addition, depending on your specific location, there may also be state and local taxes that apply to your earnings. However, the amount of taxes you owe may vary based on various factors such as your income level, applicable deductions, and other personal circumstances.

It's important to note that employers are usually required to withhold the applicable taxes from your paycheck and remit them to the relevant tax agencies on your behalf. When you file your income tax return at the end of the year, you may be eligible to receive a tax refund if you overpaid on your taxes throughout the year.

Again, while tax laws can be complex, it is generally advisable to consult with a qualified tax professional or financial advisor if you have any specific questions or concerns about your tax obligations.
If I get paid 144 if I work 12 hours every day expect the weekends how much would it take to get an studio apartment in the Bronx

The cost of renting a studio apartment in the Bronx can vary depending on various factors such as the location, building amenities, and other personal circumstances. However, based on current market rates, the average cost of renting a studio apartment in the Bronx ranges from $1,100 to $1,600 per month.

Assuming that you earn $144 per day and work for 5 days a week, your weekly earnings would be 5 x $144 = $720. If you work 4 weeks in a month, your monthly earnings would be 4 x $720 = $2,880.

Based on those earnings, renting a studio apartment in the Bronx at an average cost of $1,350 per month would represent between 47% to 59% of your monthly earnings, depending on the specific cost of the apartment. This is just a rough estimate and it is important to consider other expenses such as utilities, transportation, and other living expenses before making housing decisions.
What if I live with my mom and don’t have to worry about housing and instead save the money until I’m 21 how much would have I made

If you don't have to pay for housing and instead save your earnings, you could potentially save a significant amount of money by the time you turn 21. 

Assuming that you earn $144 per day and work for 5 days a week, your weekly earnings would be 5 x $144 = $720. If you work for 52 weeks in a year, your annual earnings would be 52 x $720 = $37,440.

Assuming you save all your earnings and do not spend any of it until you turn 21, you would have saved a total of $37,440 x 6 = $224,640 by the time you turn 21 years old. This is just a rough estimate, and it assumes that there are no taxes or other deductions from your earnings and that your wages remain the same over time. In reality, your earnings and financial situation may be subject to various changes and events over six years, so it's important to create a solid savings plan and make adjustments as necessary.
